tools.name: Elicit
tools.one_liner: AI research assistant that automates paper discovery, data extraction, and literature synthesis across 138M+ papers and 545K clinical trials.
key_features: Search 138M+ papers with natural language queries
Auto-extract structured data into comparison tables
Generate research reports with sentence-level citations
Systematic review: screen up to 5,000 papers automatically
Real-time research alerts for new publications
Chat with full-text papers via AI
tools.use_case: Systematic reviews, evidence matrices, synthesizing findings across dozens of papers. Weeks of work reduced to hours.
tools.how_to_use: 1. Enter a research question. 2. Review AI-ranked results. 3. Extract structured data into columns. 4. Generate a research report. 5. Export to RIS/CSV/BIB/PDF.

pricing_details.Pro ($29/month): Systematic review workflow, 12 reports/month, API access

tools.name: Consensus
tools.one_liner: AI-powered search engine for scientific papers with evidence-based consensus scores and inline citations.
key_features: Search 200M+ scientific papers with natural language
Consensus Meter shows agreement/disagreement across studies
AI-generated summaries with inline citations
Filter by study type (RCT, meta-analysis, etc.)
Extract key findings, methods, and outcomes
Save and organize papers in personal library
tools.use_case: Quick evidence-based answers to yes/no questions (e.g., 'Does X cause Y?') and understanding scientific consensus on a topic.
tools.how_to_use: 1. Ask a comparative or yes/no question. 2. View Consensus Meter. 3. Read AI summary with citations. 4. Explore individual papers. 5. Save to library.

pricing_details.Pro ($15/month): Unlimited answers, advanced filters, citations export

tools.name: Connected Papers
tools.one_liner: Visual tool to explore and discover academic papers through an interactive graph of related works.
key_features: Visual graph showing relationships between papers
Discover prior and derivative works from a seed paper
See citation connections at a glance
Build a literature map for any research topic
Integration with Semantic Scholar database
tools.use_case: Discovering related papers you didn't know to search for; building a complete picture of a research field from one key paper.
tools.how_to_use: 1. Enter a key paper title or DOI. 2. Explore the visual graph. 3. Click nodes to read abstracts. 4. Build your reading list. 5. Export references.

tools.name: ResearchRabbit
tools.one_liner: Free AI-powered tool to discover, organize, and visualize academic literature with personalized recommendations.
key_features: AI-powered paper recommendations based on your library
Interactive citation network visualization
Personalized email digests of new papers
Zotero integration for seamless reference management
Collaborative collections with teammates
100% free for researchers
tools.use_case: Building a comprehensive literature collection; staying updated on new publications in your field; collaborative literature reviews.
tools.how_to_use: 1. Sign up for free. 2. Add seed papers to a collection. 3. Explore AI recommendations. 4. Visualize the citation network. 5. Sync with Zotero.

tools.name: Semantic Scholar
tools.one_liner: Free AI-powered research tool from Allen Institute for AI — searches 214M papers with semantic understanding.
key_features: 214M+ academic papers across all fields
AI-generated TLDRs (Too Long Didn't Read) for papers
Semantic search beyond keyword matching
Citation graphs and influential citations
Research feeds and personalized alerts
Open API for developers
tools.use_case: Deep literature discovery; finding influential papers; getting quick TLDRs of papers before reading in full.
tools.how_to_use: 1. Search by topic, title, or author. 2. Read AI-generated TLDR. 3. Explore citation context. 4. Set up research alerts. 5. Use API for advanced workflows.

pricing_details.Free (Always): Full access to all features and API

tools.name: Scite
tools.one_liner: Smart citation assistant that shows how papers have been cited — supporting, contrasting, or mentioning.
key_features: Smart citations: see if a paper is supported or contradicted
Citation context extraction from full text
Search within citation contexts
Visualize citation relationships
Reference checker for manuscripts
Browser extension for instant citation info
tools.use_case: Verifying the reliability of a paper before citing it; checking if key claims have been supported or challenged by subsequent research.
tools.how_to_use: 1. Search for a paper or claim. 2. See supporting vs contrasting citations. 3. Read citation context. 4. Use browser extension on any publisher site. 5. Export to reference manager.

tools.name: NotebookLM
tools.one_liner: Google's AI notebook that lets you upload research documents and chat with them to extract insights.
key_features: Upload PDFs, docs, and web pages as sources
AI answers grounded strictly in your uploaded sources
Generate summaries, study guides, and briefing docs
Audio Overview: converts notes to podcast-style discussion
Inline citations linking answers to source passages
Collaborative notebooks
tools.use_case: Deep reading of multiple papers simultaneously; preparing for presentations; generating study materials from a pile of documents.
tools.how_to_use: 1. Create a notebook. 2. Upload PDFs or paste URLs. 3. Ask questions about the sources. 4. Generate structured notes. 5. Share notebook with collaborators.

pricing_details.Free: Full access, up to 50 sources per notebook

tools.name: ChatPDF
tools.one_liner: Chat with any PDF instantly — ask questions, get summaries, and extract key information from research papers.
key_features: Instant Q&A with any PDF document
Automatic paper summary on upload
Multi-PDF chat (compare multiple papers)
Highlights and annotations
Export chat history
No account required for basic use
tools.use_case: Quickly extracting key points from long papers; comparing methodologies across multiple PDFs; preparing for paper reviews.
tools.how_to_use: 1. Upload a PDF or paste a URL. 2. Read the auto-generated summary. 3. Ask specific questions. 4. Download chat transcript. 5. No sign-up needed.

tools.name: SciSpace (formerly Typeset)
tools.one_liner: AI copilot for reading research papers — explains complex concepts, math, and tables in plain language.
key_features: AI explanations of complex text, equations, and tables
Highlight any text to get instant AI explanation
Search 270M+ papers with semantic understanding
Generate literature review summaries
Chrome extension works on any research website
Multi-paper chat and comparison
tools.use_case: Understanding difficult papers outside your expertise; quickly extracting methods and results; building lit reviews with minimal manual effort.
tools.how_to_use: 1. Open a paper on SciSpace or use the Chrome extension. 2. Highlight any confusing text. 3. Get instant AI explanation. 4. Ask follow-up questions. 5. Export notes.

tools.name: Zotero
tools.one_liner: Free, open-source reference manager with powerful AI plugins for summarization and smart organization.
key_features: One-click save from any browser (journals, Google Scholar, etc.)
AI-powered PDF summarization via ZoteroGPT plugin
Smart full-text search across your library
Automatic citation style formatting (9,000+ styles)
Group libraries for team collaboration
Free unlimited storage for references
tools.use_case: The backbone of any research workflow — collecting, organizing, and citing papers. AI plugins add summarization on top of the classic tool.
tools.how_to_use: 1. Install Zotero + browser extension. 2. Click extension to save papers. 3. Install ZoteroGPT for AI summaries. 4. Generate bibliography in any citation style. 5. Sync across devices.

tools.name: Mendeley
tools.one_liner: Elsevier's reference manager with AI-assisted paper discovery and academic social network features.
key_features: AI-powered paper recommendations
Academic social network — follow researchers
PDF annotation and highlighting
Automatic metadata extraction from PDFs
MS Word plugin for in-text citations
2GB free cloud storage
tools.use_case: Managing references while also discovering new papers and connecting with researchers in your field.
tools.how_to_use: 1. Import PDFs or search Mendeley catalog. 2. Organize into folders. 3. Annotate PDFs. 4. Use Word plugin to cite while writing. 5. Discover new papers via AI suggestions.

tools.name: Perplexity AI
tools.one_liner: AI-powered answer engine with real-time web search, academic mode, and cited responses.
key_features: Real-time web search with cited sources
Academic mode focuses on scholarly sources
Deep Research: 30-min automated deep dives
Follow-up questions to drill into topics
File and image upload for analysis
Spaces for organizing research threads
tools.use_case: Getting a quick, cited overview of any topic; preliminary research before diving into full papers; fact-checking claims with sources.
tools.how_to_use: 1. Type your research question. 2. Switch to Academic focus. 3. Read cited answer. 4. Click sources to verify. 5. Use Deep Research for comprehensive reports.

pricing_details.Pro ($20/month): Unlimited Pro searches, Deep Research, file uploads

tools.name: Research Rabbit
tools.one_liner: Spotify for research papers — free AI tool that maps citation networks and delivers personalized paper recommendations.
key_features: AI maps connections between papers visually
Daily email digest of newly published relevant papers
Free Zotero two-way sync
Team collections for shared research projects
Author network exploration
100% free — no paywalls
tools.use_case: Staying up-to-date with new publications; discovering key prior works you might have missed; collaborative literature management with a team.
tools.how_to_use: 1. Add papers to a collection. 2. Let AI suggest related papers. 3. Explore the network graph. 4. Set up daily email digest. 5. Invite collaborators.

pricing_details.Free (Always): All features, unlimited use, Zotero sync

tools.name: Jenni AI
tools.one_liner: AI writing assistant built specifically for academic writing — generates, paraphrases, and cites in real time.
key_features: AI autocomplete trained on academic writing style
In-text citations from 200M+ papers (APA, MLA, etc.)
Paraphrase and rewrite selected text
PDF and document chat for reference
Plagiarism-conscious writing suggestions
LaTeX export for journal submission
tools.use_case: Overcoming writer's block in academic papers; getting AI-generated first drafts with proper citations; paraphrasing without plagiarism concerns.
tools.how_to_use: 1. Start a document in Jenni. 2. Type your ideas — AI completes sentences. 3. Add citations via search. 4. Paraphrase selected text. 5. Export to Word or LaTeX.

tools.name: Grammarly
tools.one_liner: AI writing assistant for grammar, clarity, tone, and style — now with generative AI for academic rewriting.
key_features: Real-time grammar and spelling correction
Clarity and conciseness suggestions
Tone adjustment for academic writing
Plagiarism detection (Premium)
AI rewrite and paraphrase suggestions
Works in browser, Word, and Google Docs
tools.use_case: Polishing academic manuscripts for clarity and correctness; catching subtle errors before submission; non-native English speakers improving writing quality.
tools.how_to_use: 1. Install browser extension or desktop app. 2. Write or paste your text. 3. Review suggestions in real-time. 4. Accept or reject changes. 5. Check for plagiarism (Premium).

tools.name: Overleaf
tools.one_liner: Cloud-based LaTeX editor with real-time collaboration — the standard platform for scientific manuscript writing.
key_features: Real-time collaborative LaTeX editing
10,000+ academic templates (IEEE, APA, Nature, etc.)
AI writing assistant (grammar, rewrite)
Git integration for version control
Direct journal submission (many publishers)
Compile and preview PDF instantly
tools.use_case: Writing conference papers, journal articles, and theses collaboratively; managing complex LaTeX documents for STEM publications.
tools.how_to_use: 1. Choose a template for your target journal. 2. Write in LaTeX editor. 3. Real-time preview on the right. 4. Invite co-authors. 5. Submit directly to journal.

pricing_details.Standard ($21/month): Unlimited collaborators, Dropbox sync, track changes

tools.name: Litmaps
tools.one_liner: AI-powered citation map tool that discovers related papers and tracks new publications automatically.
key_features: Seed map: build citation network from one paper
Discover mode: finds papers you probably missed
Alert system for new papers in your map
Chronological view of research evolution
Export to Zotero, Mendeley, or CSV
Visual exploration of literature landscape
tools.use_case: Comprehensive literature mapping; tracking how a research field has evolved; ensuring systematic coverage for literature reviews.
tools.how_to_use: 1. Enter a seed paper. 2. Explore generated map. 3. Enable alerts for new papers. 4. Add more seed papers to expand map. 5. Export selected references.

tools.name: Scholarcy
tools.one_liner: AI flashcard generator that summarizes research papers, extracts key claims, and builds reference libraries.
key_features: Auto-generate article summaries and flashcards
Extract key concepts, contributions, and limitations
Identify and link all cited references
Convert papers to structured summary tables
Browser extension for instant summarization
Build a searchable personal summary library
tools.use_case: Rapidly screening large numbers of papers; extracting structured data from papers; building a quick-reference library of research summaries.
tools.how_to_use: 1. Upload PDF or use browser extension. 2. AI generates structured flashcard. 3. Review extracted claims. 4. Save to personal library. 5. Export to Zotero/Mendeley.

tools.name: Research Buddy
tools.one_liner: AI assistant for academic research that finds, summarizes, and explains papers from across the web.
key_features: Real-time paper search across multiple databases
One-click AI summaries of any paper
Generates literature review outlines
Explanation of jargon in plain language
Citation list auto-generation
Works with Google Scholar, PubMed, and more
tools.use_case: Getting up to speed quickly on an unfamiliar research area; generating a first draft literature review outline.
tools.how_to_use: 1. Type your research topic. 2. Browse AI-curated paper list. 3. Click any paper for instant summary. 4. Generate a lit review outline. 5. Export references.

tools.name: QuillBot
tools.one_liner: AI paraphrasing and summarization tool widely used by academics to improve writing clarity and avoid plagiarism.
key_features: 7 paraphrasing modes (Standard, Academic, Fluency, etc.)
Summarizer: condense long papers into key points
Grammar checker and proofreader
AI co-writer for draft generation
Citation generator (APA, MLA, Chicago)
Plagiarism checker
tools.use_case: Paraphrasing source material for academic use; summarizing reference papers; improving clarity and flow of academic prose.
tools.how_to_use: 1. Paste text into QuillBot. 2. Choose paraphrase mode (Academic recommended). 3. Review and accept suggestions. 4. Use Summarizer for long papers. 5. Generate citations.

pricing_details.Premium ($9.95/month): Unlimited paraphrase, all modes, plagiarism checker
